subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Race track board** (modular, double-sided sections)
- **Over 200 custom dice** in 8 different colors, each with unique face distributions
- **Player runner meeples** (1 per player)
- **Player boards** (for organizing your dice pool)
- **Money tokens** (coins)
- **Fan tokens** (movement points)
- **Draw bags** (1 per player)
- **Card display holders** for die ability cards
- **8 sets of ability cards** (each color has multiple card options for variety)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Assemble the **race track** by connecting modular track sections. Choose a track layout or use a suggested one from the rulebook.
2. For each of the 8 die colors, choose **one ability card** and place it in the card display. This determines what each color of die does this game.
3. Place the matching colored dice near their ability cards to form the **market supply**.
4. Place money and fan tokens in a general supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes a **player board**, a **draw bag**, and a **runner meeple**.
2. Each player starts with **9 basic gray dice** — place them all in your draw bag.
3. Place your runner on the **starting space** of the race track.
4. Each player draws their starting hand of dice from their bag (typically 9 to start).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Cubitos is a dice-building racing game. You build a pool of custom dice by purchasing new dice from the market, then roll them push-your-luck style to generate money and movement. Move your runner along the race track — **first player to cross the finish line wins**.

The twist: rolling too aggressively can result in a **bust**, leaving you with nothing for the round.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
All players act simultaneously each round through these phases:

### 1. Roll Phase (Simultaneous, Push-Your-Luck)
- Draw all dice from your bag.
- Roll all your dice at once.
- Dice showing a **hit** (colored symbol) are set aside as successes. Dice showing **blank** (gray face) are failures.
- **Decision**: You may **stop** and keep your hits, OR **re-roll** all blank dice for more hits.
- If after any re-roll you have **zero hits among your re-rolled dice** (all blanks), you **bust** and lose ALL hits from this round.
- You may keep pushing and re-rolling blanks as many times as you want until you stop or bust.

### 2. Run Phase (In turn order)
In player order (determined by position on the track, leader goes first):
- Spend your **hit results** to gain their effects (money, fans/movement, special abilities).
- Use **fans** to move your runner along the race track.
- Use **money** to buy new dice from the market and add them to your discard pile.

### 3. Cleanup
- All rolled dice (used or not) go to your **discard pile**.
- When your bag is empty, put all discarded dice back into your bag (like reshuffling a deck).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Buying Dice
- Each die color has a **cost** listed on its ability card.
- Pay the cost in money, then take one die of that color from the supply and add it to your discard pile.
- You may buy **multiple dice** per turn if you have enough money.

### Movement
- Spend **fan tokens** (earned from die hits) to move spaces on the track.
- Some track spaces have **bonuses** (free money, free dice, extra movement) or **penalties**.

### Die Abilities
- Each colored die has a unique ability defined by the card chosen during setup.
- Some dice give money, some give fans, some let you manipulate dice, some attack opponents.
- Since ability cards are chosen during setup, every game plays differently.

### Track Bonuses
- Landing on or passing through certain spaces grants rewards.
- **Shortcut** spaces may let you skip ahead if you meet conditions.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Busting
If you re-roll your blank dice and **every single one** comes up blank again, you bust. You lose all hits from this round and gain nothing. However, as a consolation, you move your runner **1 space** forward.

### Turn Order
The player furthest ahead on the track acts first in the Run Phase. This matters for buying dice (limited supply) and for track bonus timing.

### Line of Sight
Some ability cards reference other players' positions on the track for attack or bonus effects.

### Variable Setup
With 8 colors and multiple ability cards per color, the combination of cards chosen in setup creates enormous variability between games.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
The game ends immediately when a player's runner **crosses the finish line** on the race track. That player wins.

- There is no point scoring — it is purely a race.
- If multiple players cross the finish line in the same round, the player who went **furthest past** the finish line wins.
- If still tied, the player who was ahead at the start of the round wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Don't bust early**. Losing a whole round of movement is devastating. Stop rolling when you have a decent number of hits.
- **Buy dice that give fans**. Movement wins the game — money is just a means to get better dice.
- **Keep your dice pool lean**. Too many weak gray dice dilute your rolls. Look for ways to trash gray dice if available.
- **Pay attention to track bonuses**. Plan your movement to land on valuable spaces.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Lean pool strategy**: Fewer but better dice mean more consistent hits per roll. Prioritize quality over quantity.
- **Push-your-luck math**: With more dice showing blanks, you're more likely to get at least one hit on a re-roll. Busting requires ALL blanks — the more dice you re-roll, the safer it usually is.
- **Ability card synergies**: Some die colors combo well together. Read all the ability cards during setup and plan a strategy around 2-3 key colors.
- **Track awareness**: Don't just move blindly — sometimes it's worth buying fewer dice to hit a crucial track bonus.
- **Tempo**: If you're behind, you may need to push harder (accept bust risk). If ahead, play conservatively and let your engine carry you.
